description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a prominent gable roof covered in reddish-brown tiles. The structure features a large open entrance at the front, supported by wooden posts, and a smaller annexed roof section extending over the entryway. The walls are plain and white, with minimal ornamentation, and there are a few small, rectangular windows along the sides. The floor at the entrance appears to be tiled or paved, suitable for livestock movement. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and ventilation for housing cattle.
description: A rectangular cowshed with a red-tiled gable roof, white walls, and a large open entrance supported by wooden posts. The design is simple and functional, with minimal windows and a paved entry area.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the main open entrance, annexed roof, and supporting posts, with a human figure for scale.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, plain wall, tiled roof, and the open entry area with visible floor tiling.
description_view_2: Rear-left view displaying the full expanse of the gable roof and the solid back wall with no visible openings.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the length of the building, the continuous gable roof, and a small side opening near the corner.
attribute_door_style: large open entryway with no visible door, supported by wooden posts
attribute_window_presence: two small rectangular windows on the front side
attribute_ventilation_features: open eaves and large open entrance
